svg.title: Warden agent economy: component architecture (with PoI generation)
svg.desc: Updated component diagram of Warden's agent economy stack. Left side: Warden Chain container with Agent Registry (ERC-8004 deployment) and Proof of Inference contracts. Top right: Warden Studio for developers (agent registration) and Warden App for users. Below the App, the Wrapper deployed on LangSmith now contains a 2x2 grid of responsibilities: trust layer (sandbox plus PII guardrails), A2A invocation (calls agents), PoI generation (writes proofs to Warden Chain), and payments (internal or x402). The Wrapper invokes Internal agents (Studio-registered, internal payment) and External agents (on other chains, paid via x402). External agent discovery relies on a Warden-built Cross-chain indexer that crawls and validates ERC-8004 registries on other chains. Arrows show: Studio registers into Warden Chain; App delegates to Wrapper; Wrapper invokes both agent pools and writes Proof of Inference back to Warden Chain via the PoI generation cell; Indexer crawls Other chains.
